generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Implementation-level contracts (assign statements) should only modify existing output features or auxiliary variables.
Contracts should reference only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed within `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `int`, `real`, and `bool` for scalar type declarations in AGREE.
Ensure all identifiers are resolved and have valid types.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce unqualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope.
Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
